q: Energy demand is the term used to describe the consumption of energy by human activities.
Energy demand drives the whole energy system, influencing the total amount of energy used.
Energy demand depends on socioeconomic factors such as net capital income and development of technologies.
Energy demand forecasting is only useful for identifying current prices and has no role in environmental security.
There has been an enormous increase in the global demand for energy in recent years due to industrial development.
At present, the supply of energy is much greater than the actual demand.
The population and new technology are always expanding, leading to a year by year increase in energy demand.
The energy crisis refers to the concern that limited natural resources are diminishing as demand rises.
An energy crisis is a situation in which a nation suffers from a disruption of energy supplies connected by increasing energy prices.
Approximately ninety percent of the world's oil reserves have already been discovered.
